Game Wiki Strider657's Milkshake Mania

The complete Milkshake Mania wiki

Everything in the game, documented — blending and special outcomes, all 60 flavors and 29 combos, shops and staff, 40 upgrades, 10 location types, 35 countries, prestige, the supply chain, research, achievements, and every setting. Pick a topic from the sidebar to dive in.

What's new

Version 1.5 — "Shake the World"

Your empire goes global. The headline additions:

  • Locations+ — every location is now 5 real-world city sites (weighted toward bigger markets), with rescout and relocate, plus a Global Reach panel. See world & locations.
  • City events — street festivals, heat waves, inspections, and roadworks hit cities you occupy, swinging those sites' income for a few days.
  • Toppings & pairings — unlock toppings, slot which are active, and line up named pairings (Turtle Shake, Campfire S'more…) for extra bonuses.
  • Marketing campaigns & chains — timed income boosts, now with 3-step chain storylines that pay a permanent brand bonus when finished.
  • Critic visits & staff veterancy — named critics deliver dramatic reputation swings from level 12; staff tiers earn tenure stars worth up to +10% income.
  • Fair play & crash reports — authenticated saves, bounded mod power, legacy-import money caps, and a Minecraft-style crash screen that actually verifies your save data.
  • Polish pass — dozens more real-world cities (including smaller US spots), a full-screen Locations view, Achievements split into 9 themed subtabs, a hardened dev console (gameplay commands are dev-build only), Chrome-mobile layout fixes, and 17 more modding hooks plus 8 new UI builders.
Also in v1.4

Version 1.4 highlights

v1.4 is the modding update. The headline additions:

  • Mods — install .zip mods from the main menu. Asset mods add content with plain data; code mods run at set hook points (with your consent); library mods share code other mods build on. Everything applies on reload, no rebuild. See the modding overview.
  • Research is a skill tree — each track (Production, Marketing, Operations) holds 2–4 trees you work through by tier, 88 one-time unlocks in all, plus a money-funded Flavor Lab (37 more) that opens at level 1. Open it full screen for room to plan.
  • Mobile pass — a proper phone layout: 100% GUI scale by default, no top/bottom clipping, and a full-screen music player.
  • Display Scale, front and center — resize the whole game from Settings (browser zoom is now off, so it can't fight the layout).
  • Fixes & polish — achievement targets matched to their descriptions and sorted by difficulty, sidebar tooltips and dropdowns no longer clip, a longer interactive tutorial, and a low-cash helper banner.
Also in v1.3

Version 1.3 highlights

v1.3 is the largest update yet. The headline additions:

  • Prestige ("Rebrand") — at Level 20, reset your franchise for a permanent income multiplier and prestige tokens. The priciest flavors and countries are gated behind prestige ("Requires Prestige N").
  • Eternal upgrades — a prestige-token shop of perks that survive every reset.
  • Ingredients & supply chain — every blend consumes Milk, Sugar, Fruit & Ice; run dry and passive income stops until you restock. Assign staff to Production / Management / Restock roles.
  • 60 flavors — 6 new mythic-tier flavors (Meteor Marshmallow, Wormhole Walnut, Pulsar Peppermint, Comet Caramel Crunch, Asteroid Almond Fudge, Eclipse Espresso) and a second seasonal flavor per event (Sweetheart Swirl, Coconut Sunburst, Candy Corn Crunch, Gingerbread Cocoa).
  • Daily Special & login streak, a steeper late-game economy, and quality-of-life polish.
  • Auto… Auto-Assign — a per-save toggle that keeps your employee roles balanced automatically as you hire, with a matching achievement for switching it on.
  • Livelier customer reviews — reviews now combine more aspects with a far wider range of conjunctions, and middling reviews can weigh an upside against a downside ("great shakes, but a long wait"), so the feed reads far less repetitively. Still generated entirely from your real business metrics.
  • Gentler pacing — all income runs ~11% slower to lengthen the curve, plus slimmer sidebar tab-group headers.
Basics

Getting started

The core loop: blend shakes, reinvest the cash, level up, and automate.

1 · Blend

Load flavors into the blender and click to blend a milkshake — or flip on Auto Blend (top-left button) to let it run hands-free. Add up to 4 blenders to blend in parallel.

2 · Earn

Manual blends pay out instantly; employees and locations add passive income every second. Passive income is what carries the late game.

3 · Level up

Almost everything you do earns XP. Levels unlock new systems — shops, employees, research, price control, special upgrades, and global expansion.

4 · Expand

Reinvest into upgrades, new flavors, locations, and — from Level 15 — countries, each multiplying all of your income.
